title: Life's Trades.
content:
It's such a little thing to weep,
    So short a thing to sigh;
    And yet by trades the size of these
    We men and women die!
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Life's Trades."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
